events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes multiple serious hazards: cords across walkways, missing guardrails, blocked egress/extinguishers, improvised scaffolds, unattended open holes. Work is in-progress. This is clearly a fail with critical/high risk.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 30,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Electrical cords run across walkways creating trip and electrical hazards",
    "Missing guardrails at elevated/open edge locations creating fall hazards",
    "Blocked egress paths preventing emergency evacuation",
    "Blocked or inaccessible fire extinguishers reducing emergency response capability",
    "Improvised/non-engineered scaffold in use without proper assembly or inspection",
    "Unattended open holes without covers, guards, or signage creating fall-through hazards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop affected work immediately and secure the area",
    "Reroute cords overhead or use cord covers/ramps; remove from walkways",
    "Install compliant guardrails or temporary fall protection before resuming work",
    "Clear all egress paths and extinguisher access immediately",
    "Remove improvised scaffold from service; erect scaffold per 1926 Subpart L by qualified personnel and inspect before use",
    "Cover open holes with rated covers secured against displacement, or post guardrails and signage; never leave open holes unattended",
    "Conduct site housekeeping sweep and pre-work hazard assessment before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "Multiple serious and immediate hazards are present simultaneously: trip/electrical, fall, egress, fire-protection, scaffold, and open-hole hazards. These violate OSHA general duty and 1926 Subpart L requirements and present imminent danger to workers. Work cannot continue until controls are in place.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause, 29 USC 654(a)(1)-(2)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L - Scaffolds",
    "29 CFR 1926.501 - Fall Protection",
    "29 CFR 1910.37 - Maintenance, safeguards, and operational features for exit routes",
    "29 CFR 1910.157 - Portable fire extinguishers"
  ]
}
